fix: .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps (Vercel silently skips @vitejs/plugin-react on peer conflict)
fix: .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps (Vercel silently skips @vitejs/plugin-react on peer conflict)
fix: .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps (Vercel silently skips @vitejs/plugin-react on peer conflict)

revert: remove .npmrc (caused ETARGET on @radix-ui version resolution)

Without a lockfile, prefer-offline=false made npm resolve caret ranges to
non-existent latest versions. Removing .npmrc lets Vercel install from
package.json ranges normally.

fix: .npmrc force public registry + no offline cache for Vercel build
fix: .npmrc force public registry + no offline cache for Vercel build

fix: move build-time deps (vite, plugin-react, typescript) to dependencies

Vercel's production build only installs , skipping
devDependencies. This dropped nitro, @vitejs/plugin-react, vite, typescript
from the build, causing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND. Moved all build-time packages
to dependencies so they are always present.

fix: move nitro to dependencies (Vercel skips devDeps on prod build)

npm install on Vercel reported 'added 293 packages' but nitro was missing,
causing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND on 'nitro/vite'. Moving nitro from
devDependencies to dependencies so it is always installed in the build.

fix: move nitro to dependencies (Vercel skips devDeps on prod build)

npm install on Vercel reported 'added 293 packages' but nitro was missing,
causing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND on 'nitro/vite'. Moving nitro from

fix: vercel.json installCommand npm install (resolves full dep tree on Vercel)
fix: vercel.json installCommand npm install (resolves full dep tree on Vercel)
fix: vercel.json installCommand npm install (resolves full dep tree on Vercel)

fix: regenerate package-lock.json with full dep tree (nitro, plugin-react present)

Previous --package-lock-only run produced an incomplete lock that omitted
nitro and @vitejs/plugin-react, causing Vercel's npm ci to skip them.
Regenerated via a full npm install so the lock matches package.json.

fix: remove @lovable.dev/vite-tanstack-config — inline equivalent Vite config

The scoped Lovable package could not be installed in Vercel's build env
(ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND on every deploy). Replaced it with a self-contained
vite.config.ts that wires the same production plugins directly:
@tailwindcss/vite, vite-tsconfig-paths, @tanstack/react-start/plugin/vite,
nitro (vercel preset), @vitejs/plugin-react, lightningcss transformer,
'@' alias, React/TanStack dedupe, tslib ESM alias. Dev-only/sandbox plugins
(hmr-gate, dev-server-bridge, assets-proxy, component-tagger, ssr error
loggers) are omitted — not needed on Vercel. Verified: npm run build passes
locally without the Lovable package.

chore: deploy marker in <title> to verify Vercel edge picks up new build

Adds [deploy:a5c8d24] to the landing <title> so we can confirm via curl
whether the live edge is serving the fixed bundle (env loading fix) or a
stale pre-fix build.
fix: vercel.json — explicit buildCommand npm run build after npm ci

fix: add .npmrc (public registry) + regenerate package-lock

Ensures Vercel installs @lovable.dev/vite-tanstack-config (scoped public
pkg) from registry.npmjs.org instead of failing with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND.
fix: vercel.json schema — installCommand is top-level, not under build

meta.githubCommitMessage:
fix: Vercel build — use 'npm ci' for clean install (resolves @lovable.dev scoped dep)

Forces Vercel to install from package-lock.json instead of a cached
node_modules that was missing @lovable.dev/vite-tanstack-config, which
caused the production build to fail with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND.

deployments.errorMessage: The `vercel.json` schema validation failed with the following message: `build` should NOT have additional property `installCommand`

meta.githubCommitMessage:
chore: ignore .vercel/ build output

Removes generated Vercel build artifacts from version control so future
builds don't bloat the repo or produce noisy delete diffs.

chore: ignore .vercel/ build output

Removes generated Vercel build artifacts from version control so future
builds don't bloat the repo or produce noisy delete diffs.

chore: deploy marker in <title> to verify Vercel edge picks up new build

Adds [deploy:a5c8d24] to the landing <title> so we can confirm via curl
whether the live edge is serving the fixed bundle (env loading fix) or a
stale pre-fix build.

chore: deploy marker in <title> to verify Vercel edge picks up new build

Adds [deploy:a5c8d24] to the landing <title> so we can confirm via curl
whether the live edge is serving the fixed bundle (env loading fix) or a
stale pre-fix build.

fix: 500 on ?preview_domain — load Supabase env in production server

The Nitro production server (what Vercel runs) does not auto-read a local
.env file. SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY were therefore undefined
at runtime, so the service_role admin client threw 'Missing Supabase
environment variable(s)' and every workspace lookup (findByDomain, used by
getPublicWorkspaceContent for ?preview_domain=<domain>) failed → HTTP 500
('This page didn\'t load').

Added src/lib/env.server.ts: a lightweight .env parser that loads
SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from disk ONLY when they are absent
from process.env (no-op on Vercel where they are set via dashboard). Called
from createSupabaseAdminClient() before reading the vars.

Verified locally against the built .vercel output: /?preview_domain=khane.nama.app
now returns HTTP 200 with full rendered content. Added a regression test for
select().eq().maybeSingle() (the findByDomain query path).

chore: force Vercel rebuild after 500 fix (deploy marker)

Pushes a no-op source change so the connected Vercel project re-runs the
production build and serves the fixed bundle (useOptionalWorkspace import fix
from 9e58b38) on the live edge instead of the stale pre-fix build.

fix: 500 on landing — import useOptionalWorkspace in providers/index

The merged providers/index.ts called useOptionalWorkspace() inside
useRepositories() but only re-exported it (export { ... } from ...) instead of
importing it into module scope. A re-export does not bind the name locally, so
SSR threw 'ReferenceError: useOptionalWorkspace is not defined' and the whole
landing page returned HTTP 500.

Also: moved the admin-href test out of src/routes/ (it was picked up by the
TanStack Start route scanner with 'does not export a Route'); added .vercel/
to .gitignore.

fix: landing admin button opens tenant/preview-scoped admin URL

The footer 'ورود به پنل مدیریت' link was hardcoded to /admin, so it always
opened the root platform admin regardless of which tenant/subdomain/preview
the visitor was on. buildAdminHref() now scopes the entry URL:

- ?preview_domain=<domain> → /admin?preview_domain=<domain> (keep preview)
- tenant subdomain (e.g. khane.nama.app) → relative /admin (host-scoped)
- root domain with a resolved tenant domain → /admin?preview_domain=<domain>

Added index.admin-href.test.tsx covering all branches.

fix: keep created block visible instead of refetch-clobbering it

onSettled invalidateQueries could drop a just-inserted block when the live page_blocks SELECT RLS is stricter than USING(true) (the block flashes then vanishes). Remove the forced refetch — onSuccess already adds the real row, and the realtime cms-sync channel re-fetches external changes.
fixed fake block adding
isolation and provision tests
new deploy
